# navra

> navra is a secure agentic AI framework for Rust. It combines an MCP gateway, a Rust agent SDK (navra-agent), and a multi-agent flow engine (navra-flow) in a 22-crate workspace. Security is enforced at the gateway layer — Information Flow Control, deny-wins ACLs, content safety filtering (regex + NER + ML), BLAKE3 authentication, capability tokens, and hash-chained audit logging — so agents don't need to be trusted. The SDK provides a builder API, 5 model backends (Ollama, Mistral, Anthropic, OGX, CLI), deterministic replay, and Hermes-format trace export. Flows support DAG execution, handoff routing, and mesh communication. 2400+ tests, 34 adversarial security tests, 138 Kani formal verification proofs. Apache-2.0.

Default config path: `~/.config/navra/config.toml`. Run `navra init` for interactive setup. Build requires ONNX Runtime (`onnxruntime-devel` on Fedora) and environment variables `ORT_LIB_PATH=/usr/lib64 ORT_PREFER_DYNAMIC_LINK=1`.

## Docs

- [WHY-NAVRA.md](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/WHY-NAVRA.md): What navra does differently — IFC, deny-wins ACLs, formal proofs, composable architecture, competitive comparison
- [CONFIG.md](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/CONFIG.md): Complete configuration reference — every TOML key, type, default value, and description
- [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): Project history by month — features, fixes, documentation changes
- [DESIGN.md](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/DESIGN.md): Full architecture, MCP protocol implementation, security model, defense-in-depth layers, threat model
- [TESTING.md](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/TESTING.md): Test prerequisites, how to run unit/integration/e2e tests, per-crate test counts
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): Contribution guidelines, commit conventions, DCO sign-off, code style
- [SECURITY.md](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/SECURITY.md): Vulnerability disclosure policy, scope, response timeline
- [MODELS.md](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/MODELS.md): Model integration architecture, CPU/GPU tiers, hardware profiles, ONNX and served model setup
- [ROADMAP.md](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/ROADMAP.md): Phased development plan, dependency graph, current priorities

## Architecture

- [README.md](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/README.md): Project overview, features, quickstart, workspace layout, architecture diagram
- [DISCOVERY.md](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/DISCOVERY.md): Agent and tool discovery landscape — AID, A2A, MCP Server Cards, DNS-SD
- [OPENSHELL.md](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/OPENSHELL.md): OpenShell integration for sandboxed agent execution, identity federation, A2A mesh

## Crates

- [navra-protocol](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-protocol/README.md): MCP/A2A/JSON-RPC types and upstream client transports
- [navra-model](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-model/README.md): Model backend trait with ONNX, OpenAI, and Anthropic implementations
- [navra-security](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-security/README.md): Auth, permissions, IFC, safety filters, hook pipeline, upstream tool scanning
- [navra-core](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-core/README.md): MCP server, Module trait, session management, transport layer
- [navra-agent](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-agent/README.md): Agent builder, MCP client, ReAct tool-use loop, standalone binary
- [navra-flow](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-flow/README.md): Multi-agent flows — DAG execution, handoff routing, mesh communication, mandate validation
- [navra-rag](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-rag/README.md): Hybrid FTS5+vector search, RRF fusion, cross-encoder reranking, confidence gating
- [navra-memory](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-memory/README.md): Working memory and knowledge store with FTS5
- [navra-cognitive](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-cognitive/README.md): Persona/directive/heuristic YAML loader and prompt weaver
- [navra-auth](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-auth/README.md): Authentication, authorization, identity, and permission primitives
- [navra-safety-hooks](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-safety-hooks/README.md): Content hooks and integrity monitoring for the gateway
- [navra-mcp](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-mcp/README.md): Module trait and type definitions for MCP tool crates
- [navra-openapi](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-openapi/README.md): OpenAPI-to-MCP runtime bridge
- [navra-macros](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/navra-macros/README.md): #[tool] proc macro for generating tool definitions from async functions

## SDK and Examples

- [Agent SDK Guide](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/docs/content/docs/sdk/_index.md): Building agents with navra-agent — builder API, model backends, IFC, traces, flows
- [Standalone Agent Example](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/examples/standalone-agent/): Complete CLI agent binary using the SDK
- [Agent Bundles](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/examples/agent-bundles/): Reference OCI bundle manifests (researcher, code-reviewer, security-auditor)
- [Flow Definitions](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/examples/flows/): DAG flows — review, deep-research, security-audit, improve, self-improve

## Guides

- [Integration Guide](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/docs/integration-guide.md): How to connect MCP clients to navra, persona prompts, security benefits
- [Goose Integration](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/docs/goose-integration.md): Token generation, navra config, Goose config, verification steps
- [MCP Tunnels](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/docs/mcp-tunnels.md): Anthropic and OpenAI tunnel setup and config examples
- [OWASP ASI Compliance](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/docs/owasp-asi-compliance.md): Mapping of navra controls to OWASP ASI01-ASI10
- [Example Config](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/examples/config.toml): Annotated starter configuration file

## Research

- [Security Gateway Paper](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/docs/papers/security-gateway.md): Gateway-enforced IFC, capability delegation, hash-chained audit — 138 Kani proofs
- [Formal Verification](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/formal/README.md): TLA+ and Kani harnesses, verification methodology
- [Proof Map](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/formal/PROOF_MAP.md): Traceability from TLA+ properties to Rust implementations and Kani harnesses

## Optional

- [Persona Orchestration Paper](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/docs/papers/persona-orchestration.md): Persona-driven multi-agent orchestration framework (draft)
- [Audit Blackbox Paper](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/docs/papers/audit-blackbox.md): Always-on hash-chained audit for AI agent gateways (draft)
- [Model Cards Paper](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/docs/papers/model-cards.md): Composite model cards for agentic AI registry metadata
- [Semantic Leakage Detection](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/docs/semantic-leakage-detection.md): L1/L2/L3 leakage detection with embedding models and LLM judge
- [Model Scheduling Design](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/docs/designs/model-scheduling.md): Cost-aware model scheduling and routing design
- [OpenShell Sandbox Design](https://github.com/smgglrs-ai/navra/blob/main/docs/designs/openshell-sandbox.md): OpenShell sandbox integration design
